reviews.review_description:
Last night I had to charge the headset 2 times while playing as I lost track of time, then I just decided to plug it in the charger while playing and then 4 HOURS passed without noticing. 

I missed that feeling of being so much in joy doing something I just loose track of the outside world! Just wow! 

I have a good idea of how the signal flows but couldn’t figure out why the clock is not in sync even if I am using the same clock module, then I found out I just need to trigger the restart simultaneously in all drums modules! 

Please maybe add a global sync button on  the clock module for lazy people like me :D, so whenever you connect any new synth or drum module it will be in sync.

I am missing a sampler, distortion, limiter, phaser, unison modules. Also either more drum kits or pc companion app please where you can drop your own samples. Maybe a simple keyboard controller module too! 

If it is possible to connect in with a random person in a multiplayer and start working on a patch would be a dream!

I would love to see at least few more environments where you are either in a room where you can put your racks while having windows with nice view or open space without being surrounded by a wall.

Overall I am full of joy and inspired but waiting to see future updates and what the devs will bring for us!

Thank you devs for this app!!

reviews.review_title: Interested in modular synth? Do it
reviews.review_description: I was definitely interested and now I can experience it. It helps to research the internet and study up on any modular synth to help get started. It’s a hard concept to grasp and making a decent patch will take time. That being said it’s realistic, takes practice and will be lots of fun when you get it. There’s a discord for it that’s helpful too. Hopefully this will get more recognition and users to continue updates. Thanks for bringing it to VR

reviews.review_title: Great!  I bought it again here...
reviews.review_description:
After using it for a while on Steam VR.
There aren't many amazing sound apps in VR yet, you should try this one!
https://youtu.be/bRAZ9DD_jM4
"First Look - It seems great!  "Modular Synths" are an expensive musical adventure in the real world, but here in VR you get a full set of them (with more to be added in future I assume?) for a reasonable price.
You have to follow the rules of electricity, but you aren't bound by the laws of gravity, the modules can float in the middle of cyberspace if you like."

reviews.review_title: It looked cool, but it doesn't work.
reviews.review_description: um.I bought this game thinking it would be amazing. But it doesn't seem to work at all. It's pretty idiotic. And I'm a musician,

reviews.review_title: A must have for creative minds!
reviews.review_description: This is has me smiling. It's amazing, and I'm gonna get lost in creating new sounds.

The team has done some outstanding work in this app and it is still in app lab. If you are into synth then this is a must have. It blows the Korg app away. Keep up the good work team and hopefully you will post some new tutorials soon! I only have two questions, how do delete something I added to the rack and where do I get patch codes ? Thanks! 
I’m sure at the rate this is going will be 5 stars when done!

This is properly awesome. A dream modular synth.

Full 3-D spatial audio!

No glitches so far despite some fairly challenging spatial audio experiments.

Though still slightl

This will be integrated with my 'conventional' studio...a TOP addition.

Thank you, devs!

My  quest Two  was gathering dust,,  until I found SynthVR,.  I've always been into synthesizers and owned a few (SC Pro-1, Moog) .  I saw Videos and Pics of Modular Systems   but never had a chance to use one. NOw I have my own.  4 stars only because there is so much more potential , and I'll reserve the 5th star for future upgrades (hopefully)
The stuff dreams are made of.

reviews.review_title: I have no idea what I'm doing
reviews.review_description:
...but it's fun anyways.

I am not a music artist, but this app is designed in a way that I can still understand enough to make some interesting sounds. It's intuitive, beginner-friendly, and fun. Also, the developer is very responsive and transparent about updates and features.
This is great to create electronic sounds, please keep it updated! Thanks
I have zero experience with synths. The premade tutorial modules got me going for so long that I ran out of battery in my quest 2. I was just going to test it out for a minute.

（5/10 日本語レビュー追記）
これはVR上で動くモジュラーシンセ、つまりオシレーターやフィルター等小さなモジュールを繋いで音を作り、演奏するタイプのシンセサイザーです。
現実のモジュラーシンセではお金や空間の制約で使えるモジュールが限られますが、VR上だといくらでも追加できるので気の済むまで音作りができます。
スイッチやツマミ、ケーブル穴がVRで使い易い形に一から設計されてるので触り心地がとてもよく、ぶっ通しで作曲してても自分の才能の無さ以外にストレスを感じる場面はありませんでした。
App Lab版だとQuest単体で持ち運んでどこでも音作りができるのも嬉しいです。

作曲なんかしたことがない？そういう人はDiscoverから他の人が作ったモジュールをダウンロードして演奏させてみましょう。鳴らされてる音を聞きながらそこにあるツマミを回すと……ほら、楽しいでしょ？

粘土のように音を捏ねながら作り上げていくのがモジュラーシンセの醍醐味です。クセのある楽器ですがツマミを弄ってるだけで気持ちよくなれるのでオススメです。

(review in English)
This is a modular synthesizer that can be run in VR. (It worked fine!)
A modular synth is a musical instrument that you play by connecting oscillators, filters, and other components with code to create the sound you want.
With SynthVR, you don't have to worry about where to put your rack. You don't have to spend money to add more synth modules. You can connect as many patches as you want, freely place the speakers in the space, and compose, perform, and record in 3D sound.

This synthesizer is freedom.

The patch sharing function makes it easy to download and play patches created by others and add your own arrangements. Preset drums and a sequencer are included from the start, so you can just start making some nice rhythms.

I've only had this for about an hour but this is my first real experience with modular synthesis over my digital synth and this app is really user friendly and fun to use. The menu is very simple and has a small description for every module making it easy to learn and understand.

While this app is not for everyone… for those that have an understanding of modular sound synthesis… this is absolutely a dream come true. 

Owning an actual modular synth rack is not only expensive, but takes up considerable room. Being able to “have one” that’s nearly infinitely customizable is simply legendary to nerds like me. If you want to shape your sound you won’t have to buy another $3k piece of gear, and cables to hook it up. No more “I need to buy another rack so I can fit more gear in here.” You won’t be buying any more room-heating, electricity guzzling, space-occupying racks of gear after this. Ok, some people might. 

The only improvement I would like to see is to allow free movement, and smooth turning. It’s a little bit of work to get positioned just right. While it’s not as easy to play sitting, you can. 

This app does have a steep learning curve so do not get frustrated if it takes you a while before you can even hear any sound on your own creations. Crafting sounds with a real modular synth can take some time as well… don’t be discouraged, and keep learning! Before diving in you can try out a lot of peoples creations and fiddle with knobs to experiment on something that’s already hooked up. When you are ready, you can dive in and create any sound you can dream of! 

Thanks 42tones for making this one of a kind app.
I'm new to modular synthesis, but it's hecka fun, and it all seems to work as promised.  I am a hobby musician.

This is so impressive!

I am excited to see where vr goes with music and visual art and design but this is a very solid and most impressive to me so far. The spatial audio speaker placement adds a very interesting element too. Looking forward to learning more with this app.

A modular visual builder like jitter in max would just be the icing on the cake with something like this.

Highly recommend and has allowed me to test the world of modular synths without breaking the bank.
